38.reason: The loop condition should be i < i1, because i1 is the count of terms (incremented after reading), so valid indices are 0 to i1-1. Using <= i1 accesses an uninitialized element.

47.reason: [This merging logic is flawed. It only merges consecutive duplicate exponents but fails to handle cases where same exponents appear non-consecutively after sorting (though bubble sort should make them consecutive). However, more critically, it prints a term even when coefficient becomes zero after merging, and also mishandles the last group of exponents. Additionally, it prints intermediate accumulations incorrectly due to improper loop control.]

However, more critically, it prints a term even when coefficient becomes zero after merging, and also mishandles the last group of exponents. Additionally, it prints intermediate accumulations incorrectly due to improper loop control.]
